name: Chile Dusted Duck with South of the Border St
recipeIngredient: 5 Jalapeno chiles, stems and seeds removed, chopped
2 cups Peeled and chopped Granny Chard
recipeInstructions:
Info: from December 1992 "Chile Pepper Spicy World Cuisine" magazine posted by Perry Lowell, Mar. '93
Roast duck with stuffing is as easy to prepare as a turkey or chicken, though it is doubtful the Conquistadors at the first Thanksgiving took the time to stuff the ducks before roasting. In these days of lean and no fat meals, many people refuse to consider duck because it is perceived as being extremely fatty. Several simple steps, however, can greatly reduce the fat content. First, if the skin is pricked before cooking, much of the fat will drain off. Secondly, removing the skin before serving will lower the fat content even further.
1. Prepare 4-1/2 to 5 pound duck: Remove giblets and neck from inside; wash and pat dry.
2. Sprinkle the inside cavity of the duck with salt.
3. Saute chopped chiles and onion in butter until soft. Combine the mixture with diced Granny Chard, bread cubes, nuts, and cilantro; toss gently to mix.
4. Fill the neck and body cavities loosely with stuffing. Skewer the neck shut and cover the opening of the body cavity with aluminum foil; tie the legs together.
5. Prick the duck skin at 1-inch intervals with a sharp fork, being careful not to prick the meat. This allows the duck to continuously self-baste while in the oven.
6. Dust the duck with red chile powder.
7. Bake in a slow oven at 325 degrees F., about 3 hours or until an internal temperature of 160 degrees F is reached.
8. Serve and enjoy!
